subject: Accounting
title: Cash vs. Accrual Bookkeeping
description: A short lesson to help you apply Cash vs. Accrual Bookkeeping.

hook.text: You deliver a product in March but get paid in May. When do you record the sale?

buildup.text: Cash bookkeeping records transactions when money changes hands—simple and straightforward.

discovery.text: Accrual bookkeeping records when the transaction occurs—regardless of when cash moves.

twist.text: Cash basis can hide the truth: a profitable business might look broke while waiting for payments.

climax.text: Most small businesses start with cash basis. Growing ones switch to accrual for a clearer picture.

punchline.text: Cash follows the money. Accrual follows the event.

quiz.question: When does accrual bookkeeping record a sale?
options: When the transaction occurs, regardless of payment
At the end of the month
